summary: Phase 1 security hardening regression pass for Nexus Panel backend. All 9 tests passed (login, /me, single-token revoke, logout-all, per-IP+username brute-force 429, env secret plaintext round-trip, 40x rapid /me under rate limit, unauth 401, authed project list).
critical_code_review_comments: Note: pytest.ini forces pytest-xdist LoadScope which caused module-scoped fixtures to break sequencing. Switched fixture to function-scope so each test uses a fresh token; this avoids interference from logout-all epoch bumps.
Consider adding a light note in docs: after logout-all, any previously issued token for that user is rejected with 'Session expired' (verified).
seed_data_creation: Created and cleaned up one temporary project TEST_secret_proj_* to validate env_vars plaintext round-trip. Deleted in test teardown.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Regression suite at /app/backend/tests/test_phase1_security.py covers Phase 1 auth hardening. Run with: `python -m pytest backend/tests/test_phase1_security.py -v -o addopts=''` (pytest.ini sets xdist LoadScope; must disable addopts or keep fixtures function-scoped). Brute-force test uses throwaway username 'nosuchuser_bf_test' so real admin is never locked.
rca of the issue: n/a - all tests passed. Initial run had 3 apparent failures due to test-side fixture scope (module-scoped admin_token was invalidated when the logout-all test bumped the user's token_epoch). Fixed by scoping the fixture per-test; the backend behavior itself is correct.
